title: Smoky Flaky Kenyan Bread Flatbread
description: A bitter, flaky flatbread infused with smoky flavors, perfect for festive Kenyan celebrations.

ingredients.name: gluten-free oat flour
ingredients.notes: helps make the flatbread gluten‑free friendly

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the bread flour, oat flour, salt, and sugar.
Sprinkle the yeast on top of the dry ingredients and lightly stir to mix.
Add the warm water and 1 tablespoon of olive oil; stir until a sticky dough forms.
Transfer the dough onto a floured surface and knead for about 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough back in the bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 hours or until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide it into four equal portions. Roll each portion into a 1‑mm thick circle using a rolling pin, dusting lightly with flour as needed.
Heat a tandoor or a very hot cast‑iron skillet over medium‑high heat. Brush a little olive oil onto the surface.
Place one flatbread in the tandoor; cook for 2–3 minutes until bubbles form, then flip and cook the other side for another 2–3 minutes, letting the edges blister slightly.
Repeat with the remaining flatbreads, brushing each with a thin layer of olive oil before placing in the tandoor.
Remove the flatbreads, let them cool on a wire rack for a minute, then serve hot, optionally dusted with a pinch of smoked paprika for extra flavor.
